topic: how to list items for my business on facebook marketplace

inputs.topic: how to list items for my business on facebook marketplace
inputs.struggles: they struggel. with the navigation, key words and more
inputs.context: important for the student to learn about sales and distribution

lesson.teacherObjective: After this lesson, you will have successfully created and published a listing for your item on Facebook Marketplace.
lesson.teacherExplanation:
Facebook Marketplace is a powerful tool for selling items locally. Many adults in the community use it to buy and sell daily necessities. It's a platform where people can safely buy from others in their area without hidden costs. Understanding this tool can be an advantage for your entrepreneurial journey.

Inkify, a local print store owned by Samke Jaca and Ntokozo Gwacela, has effectively leveraged Facebook Marketplace to offer printing services. They found that it helps them connect with potential clients around KwaZulu-Natal without spending any money. This can be particularly useful if you are starting with zero capital.

Learning to list items on Facebook Marketplace empowers you to reach a broader audience and improve your sales skills. As a student, you can sell items or services you create, such as baked goods, crafts, or tutoring services. By mastering this tool, you can start a small business right from your phone, increasing your understanding of sales and distribution.
teacherDiscussionQuestions: What businesses in our community use Facebook Marketplace?
How can you ensure safety when meeting buyers?
What product would you like to list on Marketplace?
lesson.teacherLocalExample: One student, inspired by 1LT Bakery's success in baking bread with zero capital, could consider listing home-baked goods on Facebook Marketplace to connect with nearby buyers.
teacherTimeGuide: 15 min: Step-by-step guide to create listing
5 min: Discussing potential businesses to list
5 min: Peer sharing of listing ideas
lesson.taskIntro: You will have your products listed and visible to potential buyers.
lesson.taskReflection: How do you think showcasing your products online can impact your sales?

steps.teach: Open the Play Store app on your Android device. This is where you find and insta
Use the search bar to find the Facebook app. Type 'Facebook' and tap the search
Find the Facebook app in the search results. Tap 'Install' to download it on you
Wait for the Facebook app to install. Once done, tap 'Open' to launch the app.
Open Facebook from your device. This is the home screen where you see your news
Tap on the Marketplace icon to access the Marketplace section.
To create a listing, tap on 'Create New Listing'. Fill in the details such as photo, price, and title.

lesson.teacherPlanHTML:
Learning Objective

Listing Items on Facebook Marketplace

After this lesson, you will have successfully created and published a listing for your item on Facebook Marketplace.

✏ Write on the Board
Write these before class starts — students copy them down
  • Download Facebook app
  • Explore Marketplace section
  • Create new listing
  • Include details: photo, title, price
  • Contact method: WhatsApp

2Explain to Students

Read this aloud — then explain in your own words

Facebook Marketplace is a powerful tool for selling items locally. Many adults in the community use it to buy and sell daily necessities. It's a platform where people can safely buy from others in their area without hidden costs. Understanding this tool can be an advantage for your entrepreneurial journey.

Inkify, a local print store owned by Samke Jaca and Ntokozo Gwacela, has effectively leveraged Facebook Marketplace to offer printing services. They found that it helps them connect with potential clients around KwaZulu-Natal without spending any money. This can be particularly useful if you are starting with zero capital.

Learning to list items on Facebook Marketplace empowers you to reach a broader audience and improve your sales skills. As a student, you can sell items or services you create, such as baked goods, crafts, or tutoring services. By mastering this tool, you can start a small business right from your phone, increasing your understanding of sales and distribution.

3Discussion Questions

  1. 1What businesses in our community use Facebook Marketplace?
  2. 2How can you ensure safety when meeting buyers?
  3. 3What product would you like to list on Marketplace?

4Local Example

One student, inspired by 1LT Bakery's success in baking bread with zero capital, could consider listing home-baked goods on Facebook Marketplace to connect with nearby buyers.
Time Guide — 30 minutes
5 minIntroduction to Facebook Marketplace
15 minStep-by-step guide to create listing
5 minDiscussing potential businesses to list
5 minPeer sharing of listing ideas
5 minFinal review and questions
